title: CLI: /subscribe command
description:
Add /subscribe slash command to the CLI chat loop.

What: the user types /subscribe in the chat REPL; the CLI sends subscribe_request to relay and displays the invoice.

Algorithm:
1. In cli/src/commands.rs, locate the strip_prefix block above handle_slash() (around line 1693) where /pay, /balance, /receive are handled.
2. Add: if line.trim() == '/subscribe' { ... }
3. Inside: send JsonRpcRequest { method: rpc_methods::SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST, params: SubscribeRequestParams { duration_days: 30 } } via relay_tx.
4. Await the response on relay_rx (use the same request-response correlation as other CLI commands).
5. On success (SubscribeInvoiceResult): print:
   'Subscription invoice:'
   '  Address: <address>'
   '  Amount:  <amount_zatoshi> zatoshi (<amount_zec> ZEC)'
   '  Expires: <expires_at>'
   '  Send ZEC to the above address to activate your subscription.'
6. On error SUBSCRIPTION_REQUIRED: print 'A pending invoice already exists. Check your previous invoice or wait for it to expire.'
7. On METHOD_NOT_FOUND: print 'This relay does not support subscription payments.'
8. Handle subscription_active notification in the incoming message loop: when received, print 'Subscription activated. Expires: <expires_at>'. Update any local subscription display.

Defensive rules:
- The address displayed to the user is a public UA — no key material.
- Do not log the full invoice to debug output.
- Duration in output should be in human-readable ZEC (divide by 1e8), not raw zatoshi.

Tests:
- Manual/integration: in a test relay, send /subscribe and verify invoice is printed.
acceptance_criteria: cargo build --bin nie passes; /subscribe prints invoice in the chat REPL
